title: astragalus sabulonum
intro: Astragalus sabulonum, also known as the sand pea or sand milk-vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to dry, sandy areas in North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The medicinal properties of Astragalus sabulonum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced inflammation. Astragalus sabulonum is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall health and resilience.
